frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: recent percepts bias current ones across consecutive perceptual episodes

frameworks.name: object identity / object continuity
frameworks.one_line: perceived identity links representations across moments to support continuity

core_question: How does the visual system represent perceptual continuity across moments via serial dependence?
key_assumptions: vision must reconcile change with apparent stability
prior stimuli leave residual influence on current percepts
object identity guides binding across episodes
key_findings: signatures of serial dependence appear across many diverse visual tasks
findings span the past decade of paradigms with disparate results
interpretations: serial dependence reflects an anticipatory mechanism linking consecutive percepts via object identity
apparently contrasting results share common patterns suggesting a unifying account
limitations_noted: disparate findings and interpretations lack a general picture
several questions about the phenomenon remain unanswered
